response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a seasoned full-stack engineer with over 15 years of software development experience and genuine staff-level credentials, but the role demands 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions — a bar this candidate does not meet. AI-related work appears only in the current role (started April 2025, roughly one month of tenure), limited to MCP tooling and agentic workflow infrastructure with no mention of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph or CrewAI. Critically, Python — a primary required language — is entirely absent from the skills section and employment history, replaced by TypeScript, DLang, and Node.js. The depth and specialization the role requires simply isn't present in this profile.
strengths: Staff-level IC trajectory across multiple employers with demonstrated technical leadership, mentoring, and knowledge-sharing (workshops, Selenium courses, Refactoring Dojos)
Current role at re:cinq explicitly involves MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure, showing entry into the GenAI tooling space
Strong TypeScript and AWS CDK experience (Optilyz) with production-scale systems handling millions of operations, indicating cloud-platform familiarity
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills list and entire employment history, yet it is a primary required language for this role
Fewer than two months of any AI-adjacent work; zero demonstrated experience with RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation, or multi-agent frameworks
No evidence of customer-facing technical leadership, discovery sessions, or consulting engagement — core to the forward-deployed nature of this position
